contract: Two layers. (1) Identity derivation — PURE: actor_id = sha256('<salt>:<raw_email>').hexdigest(), salt from env IHL_PII_SALT (dev default 'ihl-pii-salt'); auth_session.issue_magic_link hashes email.strip().lower(), but apps/api/routes/auth.py magic-link route hashes body.email RAW (no strip/lower) for the pii_access_event — ver2 inconsistency, C1 must pin one normalization. (2) Session resolution — LOOKUP: resolve_actor_id(token) -> in-memory AuthSessionStore.sessions dict (opaque secrets.token_urlsafe(32) session token -> actor_id); NOT derivable, NOT vector-able. Token extraction order: X-IHL-Session header wins over 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Gate: IHL_AUTH_BYPASS truthy -> auth off; else IHL_AUTH_REQUIRED truthy -> 401 AUTH_REQUIRED without valid session.
negative_tc_guidance: TC-a: hash vectors above must match ver3 reimplementation byte-for-byte (salt explicit). TC-b: GET /auth/session without token -> 401 AUTH_REQUIRED. TC-c: unknown/expired session token -> 401. TC-d: X-IHL-Session takes precedence over Authorization Bearer when both sent. TC-e (attribution break): actor_id derived with different salt or un-normalized email must NOT equal the stored actor_id -> existing records lose attribution; assert mismatch is detected.
